\section{Performance}
\label{sec:perf}

Our real-world benchmark is a perpetual-futures DEX with 100 markets, driven by a weighted mix of oracle price updates, bulk market-maker orders, and retail taker orders, executed by Block-STM~\cite{BlockSTM} on a 60-vCPU Google Cloud instance with 235\,GB of RAM as the worker pool varies from 2 to 32 threads.
Each reported number is the mean of three runs; run-to-run spread is below 1.3\%.

\begin{table}[t]
\caption{Throughput (txns/sec, higher is better) under type and ability checking.
Slowdown vs the no-checks baseline presented in parentheses, ``waiver'' skips trusted framework code (\S\ref{sec:trusted}); ``asynchronous'' defers checks to trace replay (\S\ref{sec:async}).}
\label{tab:perf:type}
\centering
\footnotesize
\setlength{\tabcolsep}{3pt}
\begin{tabular}{|r|r|r|r|r|r|}
\hline
        & baseline & \multicolumn{2}{r|}{synchronous} & \multicolumn{2}{r|}{asynchronous} \\
workers & & no-waiver & waiver & no-waiver & waiver \\
\hline
2  & 864  & 611 ($-$29.3\%)  & 739 ($-$14.5\%)  & 513 ($-$40.6\%)  & 624 ($-$27.7\%) \\
4  & 1427 & 1026 ($-$28.1\%) & 1236 ($-$13.4\%) & 914 ($-$35.9\%)  & 1088 ($-$23.7\%) \\
8  & 1915 & 1350 ($-$29.5\%) & 1620 ($-$15.4\%) & 1427 ($-$25.5\%) & 1598 ($-$16.5\%) \\
16 & 2019 & 1427 ($-$29.3\%) & 1691 ($-$16.2\%) & 1690 ($-$16.3\%) & 1799 ($-$10.9\%) \\
32 & 1904 & 1363 ($-$28.4\%) & 1621 ($-$14.9\%) & 1633 ($-$14.2\%) & 1716 ($-$9.9\%) \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\begin{table}[t]
\caption{Throughput (txns/sec) under dynamic reference safety checking: no waiver, synchronous, in isolation (type and ability checks disabled).}
\label{tab:perf:ref}
\centering
\footnotesize
\setlength{\tabcolsep}{3pt}
\begin{tabular}{|r|r|r|r|}
\hline
workers & checks off & checks on & slowdown \\
\hline
2  & 858  & 630  & $-$26.5\% \\
4  & 1430 & 1054 & $-$26.3\% \\
8  & 1918 & 1370 & $-$28.6\% \\
16 & 2011 & 1450 & $-$27.9\% \\
32 & 1893 & 1375 & $-$27.4\% \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

Table~\ref{tab:perf:type} reports the cost of type and ability checking.
Synchronously, the combined checks cost 28--29\% of throughput, uniformly across worker counts.
The trusted-code waiver of \S\ref{sec:trusted} roughly halves this to 13--16\%, as about two thirds of executed instructions on this workload fall into trusted framework code.
The asynchronous mode of \S\ref{sec:async} trades a small tracing overhead for deferred checking on idle workers: it is slower than synchronous below 8 workers (no idle threads available), reaches parity at 8, and at 16--32 brings the cost to 10--11\%, the preferred configuration at production concurrency.

Table~\ref{tab:perf:ref} reports reference safety checking in isolation: 26--29\%, again uniform across worker counts.
While the reference checker is completely functional, it is not yet fully optimized and does not yet support the trusted-code waiver or asynchronous deferral, and thus not yet live in production.
The type-check numbers above set a reasonable expectation for what optimizing and extending these mechanisms should recover.
